POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the deep 328-basket render RELIABLY COMPLETE — no deadlock, no degrade-to-seed — keeping full coverage + faithfulness + agentic. Per the completed speed investigation (/home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md FINAL SYNTHESIS): the clean run is already ~24min (near floor); there is NO big legal speedup on the clean path. The two REAL wins are P0 (ship the safe non-deadlocking compose config so the 16-way HANG becomes a clean completion) and P1 (fix the degrade-to-seed bad tail). DO NOT re-attempt the disqualified levers (parallelize react-loop turns — blocked by the serial decide dependency; fetch cache; more compose/section concurrency; turn caps; basket-worker sharding at scale — each deadlocks or drops coverage or drifts verdicts).
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (1) A FULL 328-basket agentic deep render COMPLETES with NO deadlock and NO SIGKILL, cp4_used=agentic (NOT degraded-seed), faithfulness PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0), ~328/329 baskets rendered, in ~24min. (2) A startup GUARD (assert) refuses the deadlocking config (PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS>1 or side-judge sem>=48) until a FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B has certified it — so the deadlock can never ship again. (3) The bad tail is fixed: a run that would have tripped the 900s wall on a mega-fetch no longer DEGRADES to seed (it completes agentic, even if slower). Prove each with a real run + numbers.
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE OPUS — the hands AND the eyes. Do the hard work yourself: read the exact files named, search
where needed, build, fix, run, MEASURE. Do not wait for anyone to read the code for you.

CURRENT STATE (accurate as of wheel start — verify, don't assume):
worktree /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent @ HEAD 3ff288c (2 uncommitted files from the prior run — review/commit or discard first; ~2 orphan compose procs may be alive — ignore/kill). The off-loop to_thread wrap is at multi_section_generator.py:6079 and :6130 (SHIPPED, verdict-safe). PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS default is 1 (serial byte-identical MAP+REDUCE at verified_compose.py:2846-2995). The deadlock ROOT CAUSE (from the investigation): multiplicative thread oversubscription — PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS(3) x BASKET_WORKERS(16) x ~2 inner-TPE all contending on ONE global BoundedSemaphore; the binding entailment acquire at entailment_judge.py:155 has NO timeout; `with TPE` shutdown(wait=True) re-hangs on a wedged worker. The DEGRADE tail: outline_agent.py:2054 asyncio.wait_for(wall=900s) — ANY exception/timeout sets cp4_used='agentic-degraded-seed' at :2080; a 466s mega-fetch pushed one run past the 900s wall. CONFIRMED-SAFE config (the clean 24.2min/1449.7s run used it): off-loop + BASKET_WORKERS=1 + judge cap 4-8 + PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=3, faithfulness_pass=true, agentic held, finish_outline bounced+kept looping.

YOUR ACTIONS THIS ROUND (precise; do these, in order, then MEASURE):
STEP 0: handle the 2 uncommitted files (commit good/discard). STEP 1 (P0 — ship the safe config + guard): pin the CONFIRMED-SAFE compose config (off-loop ON, PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS=1, side-judge cap in 4-8 band, PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=3) in the launch path, and ADD A STARTUP ASSERT that refuses to run if BASKET_WORKERS>1 or side-judge-sem>=48 unless an env flag proves a full-328 verdict-identity A/B has passed. Do NOT add a timeout to the entailment acquire (entailment_judge.py:155) — it would fail-closed -> drop sentences -> faithfulness violation. Do NOT ship the judge-cap clamp. STEP 2 (P1 — kill the degrade tail): make the outline NOT degrade-to-seed when it would trip the 900s wall — raise/park the outline wall so a long-but-legitimate run COMPLETES agentic (quality-preserving even if slower), and/or handle a single mega-fetch gracefully. NO fetch-cap, NO turn cuts (those drop coverage). Prove cp4_used stays 'agentic'. STEP 3 (prove it): run the FULL 328-basket deep render on data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json end-to-end — confirm it COMPLETES (no hang), cp4_used=agentic, faithfulness PASS, ~328 baskets, wall ~24min, and (optionally) the RACE number. Commit each step with real numbers. Faithfulness is the hard gate; keep it general.

RULES:

  aggressively, commit several times, push as far as you safely can. Do NOT squeeze one tiny change

(3) The bad tail is fixed: a run that would have tripped the 900s wall on a mega-fetch no longer DEGRADES to seed (it completes agentic, even if slower). Prove each with a real run + numbers.) with a real number this round if at all possible.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

143	    this bounds the WHOLE call so one hung judge POST can never freeze the run. Runs the POST on a
144	    worker thread and waits at most ``total_s``. On timeout the client is force-closed (so the worker's
145	    hung read errors out and the thread exits) and ``concurrent.futures.TimeoutError`` is re-raised for
146	    the caller's bounded retry to reopen a fresh connection. Returns the ``httpx.Response`` on success.

149	    cap (``acquire_judge_slot``) so at most ``PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` provider POSTs are in
150	    flight at once — the per-claim burst can no longer 429-storm the mirror-chain lead host. The slot is
151	    held only for the duration of THIS POST (not the caller's retry backoffs) and is released on every
152	    exit path, so a timed-out call frees its slot when the total-deadline force-closes it. Transport-
153	    only: the verdict logic + the fail-closed sentinel are unchanged.

653	    **_ignored: Any,
654	) -> ToolResult:
655	    """The retrieval pipe as a ReAct tool. Steps (design §): per-todo query derivation
656	    (scope-anchored, ``_is_status_leak`` guard) -> ``run_live_retrieval(anchor_seed=False)`` ->
657	    ``merge_retrieval_results`` -> offset-renumber (hard assert) + URL-dedup vs existing ->
658	    S2 stamp pass (chrome delete + topic-judge fail-open) -> fold into ``ev_store`` -> disclosed
659	    ``ToolResult``. One retrieval in flight at a time (§8.4 — this coroutine is awaited, never
660	    fired concurrently by the driver)."""
661	    aspect_text = str(aspect or "").strip() or str(section or "").strip()
662	    if not aspect_text:
663	        return ToolResult(
664	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
665	            markdown="search_more_evidence requires a `section` or `aspect`.",
666	            error="missing_aspect",
667	        )
668	
669	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
670	        _is_status_leak,
671	    )
672	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
673	
674	    query_derive_max_tokens = _env_int(
675	        "PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS", PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT,
676	    )
677	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=agent_model)
678	    try:
679	        derive_prompt = (
680	            "Write ONE web-search query for the SUB-TOPIC below, kept STRICTLY within the "
681	            "scope of the RESEARCH QUESTION (carry its subject, domain and key entities; do "
682	            "NOT broaden the query into the sub-topic's generic field). Query only, one line, "
683	            "no quotes.\n\n"
684	            f"RESEARCH QUESTION:\n{workspace.research_question}\n\nSUB-TOPIC:\n{aspect_text}"
685	        )
686	        try:
687	            # Iter-6 P0 fix: same reasoning-pool bound as the checklist/decide calls above —
688	            # this call already fails open on ANY exception (including a truncation), but an
689	            # unbounded-effort reasoning prelude on a dense corpus still needlessly degrades a
690	            # one-line query derivation to the raw aspect text every time; bounding it means the
691	            # derived (scope-anchored) query actually gets used instead of the coarser fallback.
692	            resp = await client.generate(
693	                prompt=derive_prompt, max_tokens=query_derive_max_tokens, temperature=0.2,
694	                reasoning_max_tokens=_reasoning_max_tokens(),
695	            )
696	            derived = (resp.content or "").strip().splitlines()[0].strip().strip('"') if (
697	                resp.content or ""
698	            ).strip() else ""
699	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — query derivation is a small text call; degrade to raw aspect
700	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] query derivation failed, using raw aspect: %s", exc)
701	            derived = ""
702	    finally:
703	        if hasattr(client, "close"):
704	            try:
705	                await client.close()
706	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
707	                pass
708	
709	    query = derived or aspect_text
710	    if _is_status_leak(query, workspace.research_question):
711	        return ToolResult(
712	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
713	            markdown=f"Derived query was a status-leak, aborted: {query[:120]!r}",
714	            error="status_leak_aborted",
715	        )
716	
717	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import (  # noqa: PLC0415
718	        LiveRetrievalResult, run_live_retrieval,
719	    )
720	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
721	        merge_retrieval_results,
722	    )
723	
724	    t0 = time.monotonic()
725	    try:
726	        # BUG FOUND IN W1 LIVE ACCEPTANCE RUN (fixed same-iter): ``anchor_seed=False`` makes
727	        # ``run_live_retrieval`` build ``all_queries`` from ``amplified_queries`` ONLY (the
728	        # verbatim ``research_question`` is NOT auto-fired when the anchor is suppressed — see
729	        # the function's own docstring). The gap query MUST be passed via ``amplified_queries``;
730	        # ``research_question`` stays the WORKSPACE question (scope-validator anchor text) so a
731	        # scoped sub-query is validated against the real topic, not against itself.
732	        raw_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
733	            run_live_retrieval,
734	            research_question=workspace.research_question,
735	            amplified_queries=[query],
736	            domain=domain,
737	            anchor_seed=False,
738	            retrieval_deadline_monotonic=retrieval_deadline_monotonic,
739	        )
740	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a single retrieval round must not crash the loop
741	        return ToolResult(
742	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
743	            markdown=f"run_live_retrieval raised for query {query!r}: {exc}",
744	            error=str(exc)[:500],
745	        )
746	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
747	
748	    merged = merge_retrieval_results([raw_result], LiveRetrievalResult)
749	    fetched_rows = list(getattr(merged, "evidence_rows", None) or [])
750	    n_fetched_of = f"{len(fetched_rows)} of {getattr(merged, 'candidates_total', len(fetched_rows))}"
751	
752	    # THE shared fold-in seam (outline/_fold_in.py): url-dedup -> offset-renumber (hard
753	    # id-collision assert) -> S2 stamp/delete -> insert. fetch_url re-enters through the same unit.
754	    fold = await fold_in_fetched_rows(

759	    n_kept = fold.n_kept

763	        f"url-dup dropped {fold.n_url_dup}, kept {n_kept}, deleted {n_deleted} "
764	        f"(chrome={len(fold.deleted_chrome)}, off-topic={len(fold.deleted_offtopic)}) in {elapsed:.1f}s"
765	    )

773	        )

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""STEP 15: compose the REAL scoreable multi-section report from the LIVE agentic run.
3	
4	The mission's metric-(a) full-corpus gate (cp4_used=agentic on the 329-basket corpus) already
5	PASSED (docs/agentic_sweep_live_summary_s3gear329.json). What was missing was a *composed*
6	report we can score. This driver closes that gap: it runs the FULL generator
7	(``generate_multi_section_report``) with the agentic outliner ON (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 + the
8	§9.1.8 model lock) over data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json and writes report.md.
9	
10	Model it on scripts/run_honest_on_prerebuild_corpus.py (which already produced report.md +
11	multi_section_outline.json), minus the retrieval/scope machinery (the corpus is pre-built).
12	
13	Faithfulness gate (HARD): after composition, assert ZERO unverified numbers reach any
14	[CITE:ev_xxx] token in the composed report. The strict_verify lane already enforces this
15	per-section; this driver re-audits the final assembled text as an independent tripwire.
16	
17	Run (key MUST be in env):
18	    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
19	    PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py \
20	        --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json
21	"""
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import argparse
25	import asyncio
26	import json
27	import logging
28	import os
29	import re
30	import sys
31	import time
32	from pathlib import Path
33	from types import SimpleNamespace
34	
35	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
36	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
37	
38	DRB_QUERY = ROOT / "third_party" / "deep_research_bench" / "data" / "prompt_data" / "query.jsonl"
39	
40	# STEP 2 (wheel: topic-driven structure) — the section headings are now produced TOPIC-DRIVEN by
41	# the generator itself (facet outline + general research-report skeleton: PG_FACET_OUTLINE=1 +
42	# PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON=1). The prior STEP-16 approach hardcoded a clinical-archetype ->
43	# AI/labor relabel MAP here — an overfit band-aid tuned to one benchmark task. That map is GONE:
44	# the outliner emits real topical titles (Introduction / thematic bodies / Cross-Study Synthesis /
45	# Conclusions and Research Gaps) for ANY domain, so assembly renders the section titles verbatim.
46	
47	
48	def _derive_title(rq: str) -> str:
49	    """Derive a neutral report title from the research question — GENERAL, not tuned to any task.
50	
51	    Takes the first sentence/clause of the RQ, strips a leading imperative ("Please write a ...",
52	    "Research ...", "I am researching ..."), and Title-cases nothing (keeps the RQ's own wording).
53	    Falls back to a generic label. No topic is hardcoded."""
54	    import re as _re
55	    s = (rq or "").strip().replace("\n", " ")
56	    s = _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s)
57	    # First sentence only.
58	    s = _re.split(r"(?<=[.?!])\s", s, maxsplit=1)[0]
59	    # Strip common leading imperatives so the title reads as a subject, not a command.
60	    s = _re.sub(r"^(please\s+)?(help me\s+)?(write|prepare|produce|conduct|research(ing)?|"
61	                r"provide|create|complete|collect( and)?( organi[sz]e)?|i am researching|"
62	                r"i would like|i need)\b[:,]?\s*", "", s, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)
63	    s = s.strip().rstrip(".").strip()
64	    if not s:
65	        return "Research Report"
66	    # Capitalize the first letter only (preserve proper-noun casing in the rest).
67	    return s[0].upper() + s[1:]
68	
69	
70	def _load_drb_prompt(task_id: str) -> str:
71	    """Load a DeepResearch-Bench task's EXACT prompt verbatim (target/ref/criteria all key on it)."""
72	    for line in DRB_QUERY.read_text().splitlines():
73	        o = json.loads(line)
74	        if str(o.get("id")) == str(task_id):
75	            return o["prompt"]
76	    raise SystemExit(f"BLOCKED: DRB task id {task_id} not in {DRB_QUERY}")
77	
78	logging.basicConfig(
79	    level=os.environ.get("PG_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO"),
80	    format="%(asctime)s %(name)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
81	    datefmt="%H:%M:%S",
82	    stream=sys.stdout,
83	)
84	for noisy in ("httpx", "httpcore"):
85	    logging.getLogger(noisy).setLevel(logging.WARNING)
86	log = logging.getLogger("compose")
87	
88	
89	def _tier_fractions(evidence: list[dict]) -> dict[str, float]:
90	    from collections import Counter
91	    c = Counter((e.get("tier") or "T?").upper() for e in evidence)
92	    n = sum(c.values()) or 1
93	    return {k: v / n for k, v in sorted(c.items())}
94	
95	
96	# A numeric token that would be a faithfulness breach if it sat inside a [CITE:] sentence
97	# without having passed strict_verify. We audit the FINAL assembled report: any [CITE:ev_xxx]
98	# in the verified text is, by construction, already span-grounded — but we re-scan to prove it.
99	_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[CITE:(ev_[0-9a-fA-F]+|[a-z0-9_]+)\]")
100	
101	
102	def _audit_citations(report_text: str, biblio: list[dict]) -> dict:
103	    """Independent faithfulness tripwire on the FINAL assembled report.
104	
105	    strict_verify resolves every kept sentence's provenance token into a global [N] bibliography
106	    marker and DROPS any sentence whose number failed the span match. So in a faithful final
107	    report: (1) ZERO raw [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens survive (any survivor is an unverified-number leak
108	    — the exact breach the mission forbids), and (2) every [N] marker in the prose resolves to a
109	    real bibliography entry. We assert both."""
110	    leaked_cites = _CITE_RE.findall(report_text)
111	    body = report_text.split("\n\n## References\n", 1)[0]  # markers in prose only
112	    n_markers = set(int(m) for m in re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", body))
113	    biblio_nums = {int(b.get("num")) for b in biblio if str(b.get("num", "")).isdigit()}
114	    unresolved = sorted(n for n in n_markers if n not in biblio_nums)
115	    return {
116	        "leaked_cite_ev_tokens": len(leaked_cites),
117	        "leaked_cite_samples": sorted(set(leaked_cites))[:10],
118	        "distinct_bib_markers_in_prose": len(n_markers),
119	        "bibliography_entries": len(biblio_nums),
120	        "unresolved_markers": unresolved,
121	    }
122	
123	
124	async def main() -> int:
125	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)

"""P0 DEADLOCK GUARD (2026-07-12) — refuse the known-deadlocking compose config at STARTUP.

FORENSIC ROOT CAUSE (this wheel's mission; speed_investigation.md FINAL SYNTHESIS): a FULL
328-basket 16-way agentic compose DEADLOCKED — 19/20 threads wedged in ``futex_wait``, 0 progress
for 8.8 min, SIGKILLed. The cause is MULTIPLICATIVE thread oversubscription: ``PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS``
(3) x ``PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS`` (16) x the ~2 inner ThreadPoolExecutors all contend on ONE
process-global side-judge ``BoundedSemaphore``; the binding entailment ``acquire`` has NO timeout and
``with ThreadPoolExecutor`` ``shutdown(wait=True)`` re-hangs on an already-wedged worker. The small
verdict-identity A/B PASSED but did NOT exercise the full-scale semaphore + basket-worker + off-loop
interaction, so the deadlock only appeared at 328-basket scale.

THE GUARD (LAW VI, env-driven; NEVER silently mutates config): the two config dimensions that push the
compose into the un-certified deadlock regime are

  * ``PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS`` > 1  — intra-section basket map concurrency, and
  * ``PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` >= 48 — the global side-judge in-flight ceiling.

Either one, at 328-basket scale, is UN-CERTIFIED: no FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B has proven it both
completes (no hang) AND stays verdict-identical. So ``assert_safe_compose_config()`` REFUSES to start a
compose in that regime by raising ``UnsafeComposeConfigError`` — UNLESS the operator sets the explicit
certification flag ``PG_COMPOSE_DEADLOCK_CONFIG_AB_CERTIFIED=1``, which is the CONTRACT that a full-328
verdict-identity A/B has actually been run and passed. The deadlock can therefore never ship again by
accident: shipping it now requires a human to assert, via that env flag, that the A/B was done.

The CONFIRMED-SAFE config (the clean 24.2 min / 1449.7 s run used it): off-loop ON,
``PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS``=1 (serial byte-identical MAP+REDUCE), ``PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` in
the 4-8 band, ``PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS``=3 — faithfulness PASS, agentic held, no hang. That config sails
through this guard untouched (workers==1, sem<48). The guard changes NOTHING about a safe run; it only
fails-fast a KNOWN-BAD one before it can wedge the box.

Stdlib-only leaf module -> zero import cost on the safe path.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import os

# The un-certified deadlock thresholds (see module docstring for the forensic derivation).
DEADLOCK_BASKET_WORKERS_MIN = 2        # PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS > 1 (i.e. >= 2) is un-certified.
DEADLOCK_SIDE_JUDGE_SEM_MIN = 48       # PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY >= 48 is un-certified.

ENV_BASKET_WORKERS = "PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS"
ENV_SIDE_JUDGE_SEM = "PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY"
# The ONE escape hatch: an operator sets this to attest that a FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B was run
# AND passed (both no-hang AND verdict-identical). Without it the deadlock config is refused.
ENV_AB_CERTIFIED = "PG_COMPOSE_DEADLOCK_CONFIG_AB_CERTIFIED"

_TRUE = ("1", "true", "yes", "on")


class UnsafeComposeConfigError(RuntimeError):
    """Raised at compose startup when a KNOWN-DEADLOCKING config is requested without A/B certification.

    Fail-CLOSED by design: a compose must never begin in the multiplicative-oversubscription regime
    that wedged the box, unless a human has explicitly attested (via ``PG_COMPOSE_DEADLOCK_CONFIG_
    AB_CERTIFIED=1``) that a full-328 verdict-identity A/B certified it both completes and stays
    verdict-identical."""


def _int_env(env: "dict[str, str] | os._Environ[str]", name: str, default: int) -> int:
    """Read ``name`` as an int from ``env``; on unset/blank/malformed return ``default`` (fail to the
    SAFE reading — a typo must never silently DISABLE the guard by parsing as a huge/zero value)."""
    raw = str(env.get(name, "")).strip()
    if raw == "":
        return default
    try:
        return int(raw)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return default


def evaluate_compose_config(env=None) -> "tuple[bool, list[str], bool]":
    """Pure evaluation (no raise) of the compose config safety.

    Returns ``(is_safe, violations, certified)`` where ``violations`` names each un-certified
    deadlock dimension that is active. ``is_safe`` is True iff there are NO violations OR the operator
    set the A/B certification flag. Pure/side-effect-free so tests and telemetry can call it freely."""
    env = os.environ if env is None else env
    certified = str(env.get(ENV_AB_CERTIFIED, "")).strip().lower() in _TRUE
    # Basket workers default 1 (serial); side-judge sem default 4 (judge_concurrency.DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY).
    workers = _int_env(env, ENV_BASKET_WORKERS, 1)
    sem = _int_env(env, ENV_SIDE_JUDGE_SEM, 4)
    violations: list[str] = []
    if workers >= DEADLOCK_BASKET_WORKERS_MIN:
        violations.append(
            f"{ENV_BASKET_WORKERS}={workers} (>1: intra-section basket concurrency — un-certified "

            f"in-flight ceiling — un-certified; the binding entailment acquire has NO timeout)"

    is_safe = (not violations) or certified

    Call ONCE at the top of every compose entry point. Raises ``UnsafeComposeConfigError`` iff a
